message:
🐛 Disable GPU compositing layers in presentation mode to fix screen-sharing flickering (#797)

backdrop-filter: blur(), filter: blur/drop-shadow, and animated box-shadow
create GPU compositing layers that screen capture software (Teams, Zoom)
must re-render every frame, causing visible flickering even when the UI
is static.

Presentation mode already disables animations, but these CSS properties
still create compositing layers. This adds three rules:
- .glass: disable backdrop-filter: blur(12px)
- .status-*: disable box-shadow glow effects
- weather cards: disable filter: blur/drop-shadow

Complements PR #791 which eliminated DOM thrashing from skeleton/content
swaps during loading states — another source of flicker during refresh.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
